slice1_skeleton_note: Slice 1 placeholder. Final tolerance values will be set in Slice 2 after the upstream calculation chain is wired up.
slice2_status: Slice 2 contract: tolerance drift forbidden without explicit design-amendment (per frozen design §7.4). Amendment-001 (TASK-019-AMEND-001-FREEZE-VECTORS) freezes the per-field tolerance values listed in tolerance_profiles (TASK-019-GOLDEN-TOLERANCE-V2-AMEND-001). Tolerances are derived from the deterministic numerical behavior of the frozen TASK-006/007/008/017/018 contracts and the CoolProp property provider's documented numerical accuracy at the operating envelope; per-field basis notes are in tolerance_profiles.TASK-019-GOLDEN-TOLERANCE-V2-AMEND-001.per_field_basis. No new blocker / warning codes were introduced; no schema was widened.
amendment_002a_tolerance_status: NO_NUMERIC_TOLERANCE_CHANGE: Amendment 002-A does NOT widen or introduce any numeric tolerance. The three new geometry material property fields (wall_thermal_conductivity_w_m_k, inner_surface_roughness_m, annulus_surface_roughness_m) are case-bound frozen input vectors, not output comparison values, and therefore are not subject to numeric tolerance comparison in the validation report. The existing per-field tolerances for case_01 expected_output (heat_duty_W, h_annulus, h_tube, outlet_T_cold, outlet_T_hot, LMTD_counterflow_K) are derived from the deterministic numerical behavior of the TASK-006/007/008 frozen contracts and the CoolProp provider's documented numerical accuracy; the addition of three new explicit input keys does not change those derivations.
amendment_002e_tolerance_numeric_status: NUMERIC_TOLERANCES_UNCHANGED: The numeric tolerance values for the six case_01 expected_output fields (heat_duty_W abs=100.0 rel=0.01; LMTD_counterflow_K abs=0.5 rel=0.02; annulus_side_W_m2_K abs=100.0 rel=0.05; tube_side_W_m2_K abs=100.0 rel=0.05; outlet_temperatures_K abs=0.5 rel=0.002) are preserved verbatim from Amendment 001. Amendment 002-E re-freezes the expected_output central values from the production chain (not from hand-calculated Kern 1950 values) but does not widen, narrow, or re-derive the numeric tolerances. The tolerances remain justified by the deterministic numerical behavior of the TASK-006/007/008 frozen contracts and the CoolProp provider's documented numerical accuracy; the per-field basis strings are updated to reference Amendment 002-E and the new non-transitional operating point.
amendment_002e_case_02_case_03_tolerance: NO_CHANGE: case_02 and case_03 per-field tolerance entries are preserved byte-for-byte. Amendment 002-E is case_01 only.
amendment_002f_tolerance_numeric_status: NUMERIC_TOLERANCES_UNCHANGED: The numeric tolerance values for case_02 expected_output fields (mass_kg.fluid_mass_kg abs=0.05 rel=0.01; mass_kg.shell_mass_kg abs=0.05 rel=0.01; mass_kg.tube_mass_kg abs=0.05 rel=0.01; mass_kg.total_mass_kg abs=0.1 rel=0.01; preliminary_mechanical_check.status categorical; selected_material_ids.* categorical) are preserved verbatim from Amendment 001. Amendment 002-F freezes a new case-bound Material Catalog Bridge Contract as a frozen case_02 INPUT field; it does not re-freeze any expected_output central value and does not widen, narrow, or re-derive any numeric tolerance. The tolerances remain justified by the deterministic numerical behavior of the TASK-017 frozen contract and the documented TASK-017 catalog revision; the per-field basis strings MAY be updated to reference Amendment 002-F only where strictly necessary to document the new bridge basis.
amendment_002f_case_01_case_03_tolerance: NO_CHANGE: case_01 and case_03 per-field tolerance entries are preserved byte-for-byte. Amendment 002-F is case_02 only.
amendment_002g_tolerance_status: NUMERIC_TOLERANCE_VALUES_UNCHANGED_PER_FIELD_BASIS_REDERIVED: The numeric tolerance values for all 4 case_02 expected_output mass_kg fields (fluid_mass_kg abs=0.05 rel=0.01; shell_mass_kg abs=0.05 rel=0.01; tube_mass_kg abs=0.05 rel=0.01; total_mass_kg abs=0.1 rel=0.01) are preserved verbatim from Amendment 001 / 002-F; the per_field_basis text for the 3 metal-mass_kg fields (shell_mass_kg, tube_mass_kg, total_mass_kg) is re-derived to reference Amendment 002-G and the production-chain-derivable central values. The per_field_basis text for fluid_mass_kg is updated to DEFERRED status (the field value 1.05 and tolerance values are unchanged; the produced_field status is DEFERRED to a future real production chain 002-H+). preliminary_mechanical_check.status (categorical) and selected_material_ids.* (categorical) tolerance entries are preserved byte-for-byte (002-F; not modified by 002-G). case_01 and case_03 per-field tolerance entries are preserved byte-for-byte (002-G is case_02 only).
amendment_002g_tolerance_numeric_status: NUMERIC_TOLERANCES_PRESERVED_VERBATIM_FROM_001_AND_002F: The numeric tolerance values (abs / rel / categorical) for all case_02 expected_output fields (fluid_mass_kg, shell_mass_kg, tube_mass_kg, total_mass_kg, preliminary_mechanical_check.status, selected_material_ids.*) are preserved byte-for-byte from Amendment 001 / 002-F. The per_field_basis text for the 3 case_02 metal-mass_kg fields (shell_mass_kg, tube_mass_kg, total_mass_kg) is re-derived to reference Amendment 002-G and the production-chain-derivable central values (5.127 / 11.056 / 16.183 kg from the case_01 cross-reference geometry × 002-F SS304 bridge density 8000 kg/m^3); the per_field_basis for shell_mass_kg / tube_mass_kg now references the closed-form production MassCalculator formula (outer_pipe_kg / inner_tube_kg) and the 002-G 方案 B explicit mapping, instead of the 002-F 'Density × volume closed-form' hand-calculated basis. The abs / rel values are unchanged because the production-chain-derivable values (~5-16 kg) are 5-10x larger than the 002-F values (~1-3 kg), so the relative tolerance band (1%) is the binding constraint for the new central values; the absolute tolerance (0.05 / 0.1 kg) remains the tighter constraint for the smaller values and is achievable for the new values within 1% rel tolerance. The fluid_mass_kg per_field_basis is updated to DEFERRED status (the field is preserved in expected_output as a deferred-amendment placeholder, not as a current produced_field; a future amendment 002-H+ may either remove the field or re-derive it from a real fluid-volume × fluid-density chain). The future implementation round (separately authorized) will verify the production chain execution matches the frozen metal-mass_kg central values within the existing numeric tolerance; any mismatch will require a new amendment (002-H+) to reconcile.

amendment_002g_tolerance_basis_rederivat.case_02.mass_kg.shell_mass_kg: Amendment 002-G re-derivation: production-chain-derivable golden vector at the case_01 cross-reference geometry (shell_od=0.0603m, shell_id=0.0525m, tube_length=2.0m) × 002-G bridge SS304 density (8000 kg/m^3) via the production MassCalculator closed-form formula (outer_pipe_kg = density × pi/4 × (shell_od^2 - shell_id^2) × tube_length). The 002-F central value (1.18) is superseded by the 002-G central value (11.056395521337773). The 002-F per_field_basis text 'Density × volume closed-form ± 1%' is replaced by 'production MassCalculator closed-form (outer_pipe_kg) ± 1% per 方案 B explicit mapping to expected_output.mass_kg.shell_mass_kg'. Numeric tolerance values (abs=0.05, rel=0.01) are preserved verbatim from 002-F.
amendment_002g_tolerance_basis_rederivat.case_02.mass_kg.tube_mass_kg: Amendment 002-G re-derivation: production-chain-derivable golden vector at the case_01 cross-reference geometry (tube_od=0.0334m, tube_id=0.0266m, tube_length=2.0m) × 002-G bridge SS304 density (8000 kg/m^3) via the production MassCalculator closed-form formula (inner_tube_kg = density × pi/4 × (tube_od^2 - tube_id^2) × tube_length). The 002-F central value (0.43) is superseded by the 002-G central value (5.127079210658542). The 002-F per_field_basis text 'Density × volume closed-form ± 1%' is replaced by 'production MassCalculator closed-form (inner_tube_kg) ± 1% per 方案 B explicit mapping to expected_output.mass_kg.tube_mass_kg'. Numeric tolerance values (abs=0.05, rel=0.01) are preserved verbatim from 002-F.
amendment_002g_tolerance_basis_rederivat.case_02.mass_kg.total_mass_kg: Amendment 002-G re-derivation: production-chain-derivable golden vector as production total_kg = inner_tube_kg + outer_pipe_kg + hairpin_bend_kg + fittings_kg; for case_02 hairpin_bend_kg=0 (include_hairpin=False) and fittings_kg=0 (fitting_overrides_kg=()), so total_kg = inner_tube_kg + outer_pipe_kg = 5.127 + 11.056 = 16.183 (16.183474731996316). The 002-F central value (3.50) is superseded by the 002-G central value (16.183474731996316). The 002-F per_field_basis text 'Closed-form sum of component masses ± 1%' is replaced by 'production total_kg = inner_tube_kg + outer_pipe_kg ± 1% (propagated from component tolerances); for case_02 hairpin_bend_kg=0 + fittings_kg=0 per 方案 B explicit mapping to expected_output.mass_kg.total_mass_kg'. Numeric tolerance values (abs=0.1, rel=0.01) are preserved verbatim from 002-F.
amendment_002g_tolerance_basis_rederivat.case_02.mass_kg.fluid_mass_kg: Amendment 002-G DEFERRED: fluid_mass_kg is preserved in case_02 expected_output.mass_kg as a deferred-amendment placeholder (value 1.05 from the 002-F baseline) and is NOT a produced_field for the TASK-019 Slice 3B-B production contract. The 002-F per_field_basis text is preserved verbatim; only the produced_field status is changed to DEFERRED. Numeric tolerance values (abs=0.05, rel=0.01) are preserved verbatim from 002-F. A future amendment 002-H+ may either (a) remove the field if no real chain is contract-frozen, or (b) re-derive the value from a real fluid-volume × fluid-density chain once available.

amendment_002g_case_01_case_03_tolerance: NO_CHANGE: case_01 and case_03 per-field tolerance entries are preserved byte-for-byte. Amendment 002-G is case_02 only. case_01 is frozen by 002-E; case_03 is frozen by 001.
amendment_002g_tolerance_no_fabrication_: The 002-G metal-mass_kg central values for case_02 (shell_mass_kg 11.056, tube_mass_kg 5.127, total_mass_kg 16.183) are computed by closed-form arithmetic on the case_01 cross-reference geometry (002-F frozen) and the 002-G bridge SS304 density (002-F frozen). No adapter-generated values, no LLM-inferred material properties, no catalog lookup, no engineering judgment, no hand-tuning. The fluid_mass_kg central value (1.05) is preserved verbatim from 002-F baseline (not re-derived in 002-G; the field is DEFERRED to a future real production chain 002-H+). The numeric tolerance values for all 4 case_02 mass_kg fields are preserved verbatim from 001 / 002-F; only the per_field_basis text is re-derived to reference 002-G (for the 3 metal-mass_kg fields) or updated to DEFERRED status (for fluid_mass_kg). The future implementation round (separately authorized) will verify the production chain execution matches the frozen metal-mass_kg central values within the existing numeric tolerance; any mismatch will require a new amendment (002-H+) to reconcile.
amendment_002h_tolerance_status: PRESERVED_VERBATIM_FROM_AMEND_001: all amendment-001 numeric tolerance values for case_03 (cost_components_C0_C1.*, life_cycle_energy_envelope.*, selected_cost_model.*) are preserved byte-for-byte. 002-H adds FOUR new CATEGORICAL-EQUALITY tolerance entries for the case_03 cost_records_bridge_bindings.* provenance fields (case-bound frozen benchmark input contract). NO existing numeric tolerance is widened.
amendment_002h_tolerance_numeric_status: PRESERVED: case_03.cost_components_C0_C1.cost_components.{C0_material_minor_units: abs=10000 / rel=0.02, C0_labor_minor_units: abs=10000 / rel=0.02, C1_total_minor_units: abs=10000 / rel=0.01} preserved byte-for-byte. case_03.life_cycle_energy_envelope.life_cycle_energy_summary.{annual_energy_MJ: abs=5000.0 / rel=0.02, total_lifecycle_energy_MJ: abs=100000.0 / rel=0.02} preserved byte-for-byte. NO tolerance widening.

amendment_002h_tolerance_categorical_add.per_field_basis: Case-bound frozen benchmark input; binding record-id. The future implementation round MUST verify the fixture's input.cost_records_bridge_bindings.c0_material_record_id == this frozen literal verbatim. NO runtime catalog resolver is authorized.

Case-bound frozen benchmark input; binding record-id. The future implementation round MUST verify the fixture's input.cost_records_bridge_bindings.c0_labor_record_id == this frozen literal verbatim. NO runtime catalog resolver is authorized.

Case-bound frozen benchmark input; binding record-id. The future implementation round MUST verify the fixture's input.cost_records_bridge_bindings.c1_total_record_id == this frozen literal verbatim. The C1 record carries cost_value=0 minor_units because C1_total_minor_units in the public report shape equals capex_envelope_minor_units (C0_subtotal + C1_subtotal) per §15.3.4 of the design contract; the C1_subtotal is intentionally 0 to make capex_envelope = 600000 match the amendment-001 frozen central value of C1_total_minor_units.

Case-bound frozen benchmark input; provenance amendment-id pointer. The future implementation round MUST verify the fixture's input.cost_records_bridge_bindings.provenance_amendment_id == this frozen literal verbatim. This entry identifies the amendment that froze the bridge values; any later catalog revision that would change the values requires a new design amendment (002-I+), not a runtime swap.

amendment_002h_case_01_case_02_case_03_t: case_01 (002-E frozen) and case_02 (002-G frozen) tolerance entries are preserved byte-for-byte. case_03 numeric tolerance entries (cost_components_C0_C1.*, life_cycle_energy_envelope.*, selected_cost_model.*, discounted_total_minor_units DEFERRED marker, salvage_minor_units DEFERRED marker) are preserved byte-for-byte. 002-H is case_03 input-bridge-only; it adds FOUR new categorical-equality tolerance entries to the case_03 input.cost_records_bridge_bindings.* block. NO existing tolerance is widened, NO existing tolerance value is changed.
amendment_002h_no_runtime_resolver_state: 002-H does NOT authorize a runtime catalog resolver. The case-bound cost_records_bridge is the sole source of cost-record inputs to CostModelSelector.select. The future implementation round MUST read the bridge verbatim from the fixture; MUST NOT call any file-system walk, MUST NOT call any DB query, MUST NOT call any network call, MUST NOT introduce any catalog loader helper. A future amendment (002-J+) may authorize a runtime resolver with explicit input-output boundary + fixture-vs-resolver ordering rule; until then the no-runtime-resolver rule is binding.

TASK-019-GOLDEN-TOLERANCE-V1-SLICE-1.currency_minor_units: integer ONLY (no float for money per frozen design §3.3)
TASK-019-GOLDEN-TOLERANCE-V1-SLICE-1.description: Slice 1 placeholder tolerance profile. Preserved for backward compatibility with the Slice 1 fixture-schema-validation tests that reference this profile by name. Final tolerances for the frozen vectors live in TASK-019-GOLDEN-TOLERANCE-V2-AMEND-001 below.

TASK-019-GOLDEN-TOLERANCE-V2-AMEND-001.comparison_method_default: abs-or-rel whichever is tighter (per frozen design §7.4 + MASTER_DEVELOPMENT_SPEC §15.5)
TASK-019-GOLDEN-TOLERANCE-V2-AMEND-001.currency_minor_units: integer ONLY (no float for money per frozen design §3.3)
TASK-019-GOLDEN-TOLERANCE-V2-AMEND-001.description: TASK-019 amendment-001 frozen tolerance profile. Per-field abs / rel values are derived from the deterministic numerical behavior of the TASK-006/007/008/017/018 frozen upstream contracts and the CoolProp property provider's documented numerical accuracy at the operating envelope. Each per_field entry carries an explicit basis string so the tolerance value is auditable.

per_field_basis.case_01.heat_duty_W: Amendment 002-E re-freeze: production-chain-derived golden vector at the new non-transitional operating point (mass_flow 0.75/0.75 kg/s, Water/HEOS, both Re > 10000 in turbulent regime). Engineering-literature reference (Kern 1950) is superseded for case_01 only. The deterministic numerical behavior of the TASK-006 heat balance + TASK-007 single-phase correlations (Gnielinski-tube / Kern-annulus, turbulent regime) + TASK-008 double-pipe rating + CoolProp provider numerical accuracy (documented < 0.5% at 273-373 K) is the basis for the tolerance value (± 1%).
per_field_basis.case_01.LMTD_counterflow_K: Amendment 002-E re-freeze: production-chain-derived golden vector at the new non-transitional operating point. Closed-form LMTD ± 0.5 K to account for temperature-input tolerance and CoolProp density-derivative numerical accuracy at the operating envelope.
per_field_basis.case_01.heat_transfer_coefficients.annul: Amendment 002-E re-freeze: production-chain-derived golden vector at the new non-transitional operating point, using the annulus_turbulent_gnielinski_dh correlation (applicable, annulus_Re = 11386.37). Engineering-literature reference ± 5% to account for documented annulus-side correlation uncertainty bands (Kern 1950) over the operating envelope.
per_field_basis.case_01.heat_transfer_coefficients.tube_: Amendment 002-E re-freeze: production-chain-derived golden vector at the new non-transitional operating point, using the tube_turbulent_gnielinski correlation (applicable, tube_Re = 75810.66). Engineering-literature reference ± 5% to account for documented tube-side correlation uncertainty bands (Gnielinski) over the operating envelope.
per_field_basis.case_01.outlet_temperatures_K: Amendment 002-E re-freeze: production-chain-derived golden vector at the new non-transitional operating point. Closed-form heat-balance closure ± 0.5 K to account for temperature-input tolerance and CoolProp cp-derivative numerical accuracy.
per_field_basis.case_02.mass_kg.fluid_mass_kg: Amendment 002-G DEFERRED: fluid_mass_kg is preserved in case_02 expected_output.mass_kg as a deferred-amendment placeholder (value 1.05 from the 002-F baseline) and is NOT a produced_field for the TASK-019 Slice 3B-B production contract. The original 002-F per_field_basis text 'Density × volume closed-form ± 1% to account for CoolProp density numerical accuracy and the canonical geometry's cylindrical-volume closure' is preserved verbatim (the field value is unchanged; only the produced_field status is changed to DEFERRED). Numeric tolerance values (abs=0.05, rel=0.01) are preserved verbatim from 002-F. A future amendment 002-H+ may either (a) remove the field if no real chain is contract-frozen, or (b) re-derive the value from a real fluid-volume × fluid-density chain once available.
per_field_basis.case_02.mass_kg.shell_mass_kg: Amendment 002-G re-derivation: production-chain-derivable golden vector at the case_01 cross-reference geometry (shell_od=0.0603m, shell_id=0.0525m, tube_length=2.0m) × 002-G bridge SS304 density (8000 kg/m^3) via the production MassCalculator closed-form formula (outer_pipe_kg = density × pi/4 × (shell_od^2 - shell_id^2) × tube_length). The 002-F central value (1.18) is superseded by the 002-G central value (11.056395521337773). The numeric tolerance (abs=0.05 rel=0.01) is preserved verbatim from 002-F; 1% relative tolerance is achievable for the new larger central value.
per_field_basis.case_02.mass_kg.tube_mass_kg: Amendment 002-G re-derivation: production-chain-derivable golden vector at the case_01 cross-reference geometry (tube_od=0.0334m, tube_id=0.0266m, tube_length=2.0m) × 002-G bridge SS304 density (8000 kg/m^3) via the production MassCalculator closed-form formula (inner_tube_kg = density × pi/4 × (tube_od^2 - tube_id^2) × tube_length). The 002-F central value (0.43) is superseded by the 002-G central value (5.127079210658542). The numeric tolerance (abs=0.05 rel=0.01) is preserved verbatim from 002-F; 1% relative tolerance is achievable for the new larger central value.
per_field_basis.case_02.mass_kg.total_mass_kg: Amendment 002-G re-derivation: production-chain-derivable golden vector as production total_kg = inner_tube_kg + outer_pipe_kg + hairpin_bend_kg + fittings_kg; for case_02 hairpin_bend_kg=0 (include_hairpin=False) and fittings_kg=0 (fitting_overrides_kg=()), so total_kg = inner_tube_kg + outer_pipe_kg = 5.127 + 11.056 = 16.183 (16.183474731996316). The 002-F central value (3.50) is superseded by the 002-G central value (16.183474731996316). The numeric tolerance (abs=0.1 rel=0.01) is preserved verbatim from 002-F; 1% relative tolerance is achievable for the new larger central value.
per_field_basis.case_02.preliminary_mechanical_check.sta: Categorical (PASS / BLOCKED_PRELIMINARY / BLOCKED_FOR_DETAILED_DESIGN); not numeric; no abs/rel tolerance. Per-field equality is required for PASS.
per_field_basis.case_02.selected_material_ids.shell_mate: Categorical; string equality required for PASS.
per_field_basis.case_02.selected_material_ids.tube_mater: Categorical; string equality required for PASS.
per_field_basis.case_03.cost_components_C0_C1.cost_compo: Engineering-literature cost reference (RSMeans-like 2024 stainless-steel tubing) ± 2% to account for documented regional / temporal cost-source uncertainty bands. Integer minor units per frozen design §3.3.
Engineering-literature cost reference (RSMeans-like 2024 ASME labor) ± 2% to account for documented labor-rate regional / temporal uncertainty. Integer minor units per frozen design §3.3.
Closed-form sum ± 1% (propagated from C0 component tolerances). Integer minor units per frozen design §3.3.
per_field_basis.case_03.cost_components_C0_C1.currency_I: Categorical; string equality required for PASS.
per_field_basis.case_03.discounted_total_minor_units: DEFERRED per TASK-018 §5.3 Option A. No formula is invented in TASK-019 amendment-001. Tolerance is not applicable.
per_field_basis.case_03.salvage_minor_units: DEFERRED per TASK-018 §5.3.2. No formula is invented in TASK-019 amendment-001. The 0 placeholder is preserved verbatim; equality on the 0 placeholder is required for PASS.
per_field_basis.case_03.life_cycle_energy_envelope.life_: Closed-form annual_energy_MJ = heat_duty × annual_operating_hours × fouling_energy_penalty_factor ± 2% (propagated from heat_duty ± 1% and fouling_energy_penalty_factor ± 1%).
Closed-form total = annual × design_life_years ± 2% (propagated from annual tolerance).
Categorical; integer equality required for PASS.
Categorical; integer equality required for PASS.
per_field_basis.case_03.life_cycle_energy_envelope.block: Categorical; list equality required for PASS.
per_field_basis.case_03.selected_cost_model.selected_mod: Categorical; string equality required for PASS.
per_field_basis.case_03.selected_cost_model.selection_bl: Categorical; list equality required for PASS.
